message:
br-swa8k: inherit ambient cx in WorkflowRunner fallback paths

ft-swa8k: three sites in workflows/runner.rs used

    let request_cx = crate::cx::for_request();

to build a fallback cx when the caller passed cx=None. This is the same
orphan-cx pattern commit 02a599a9 fixed for WorkflowContext::send_*: a
fresh for_request() cx ignores the ambient Cx::current(), breaking
cancellation propagation for any caller that already holds one.

Switch all three to the ambient-inheriting idiom:

    let request_cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);

Sites:
- run_workflow_inner replay_capture setup (line 729) — set_decision_capture
  now runs under the parent cx when present.
- run_workflow_inner step-loop send_text branch (line 1498) — legacy
  non-cx callers (e.g. mcp_tools.rs workflow_run handler) now propagate
  MCP-request cancellation through the injector + audit write.
- run() event-loop child spawn (line 2021) — workflows spawned from
  the legacy non-cx runner loop now inherit the ambient cx so parent
  cancellation reaches the child.

Behaviour is unchanged when no ambient cx is present (for_request
fallback); this purely closes the inheritance gap. `cargo check -p
frankenterm-core --lib` on /tmp/ft-pane1-target passes clean in 3m54s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
